title: passiflora suberosa subsp. suberosa
intro: Passiflora suberosa subsp. suberosa is a species of passionflower plant native to South America, characterized by its thick, waxy, and leathery leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and insomnia. The health benefits of Passiflora suberosa subsp. suberosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve sleep quality. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant are made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or using them in teas, tinctures, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
